title: Top Spending Customers
description: Retrieve the top 2 customers based on their total order amount. Return the customer name and total spending. If multiple customers tie on total spending, break ties by lower customer_id.
sql_solution: WITH totals AS (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) total FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id), ranked AS (SELECT customer_id, total, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total DESC, customer_id ASC) rn FROM totals) SELECT c.name, total FROM ranked r JOIN customers c ON r.customer_id = c.id WHERE rn <= 2;
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

totals = orders.groupBy("customer_id").agg(sum("amount").alias("total"))
ranking_window = Window.orderBy(col("total").desc(), col("customer_id").asc())
ranked_totals = totals.withColumn("rn", row_number().over(ranking_window))

result = (
    ranked_totals.alias("r")
    .join(customers.alias("c"), col("r.customer_id") == col("c.id"), "inner")
    .filter(col("r.rn") <= 2)
    .select(col("c.name").alias("name"), col("r.total").alias("total"))
)
